description:
The original sin in compute_effective_returns is conflating
\"no values possible\" (lattice bottom) with \"not yet computed\"
(LFP iteration state). The current code uses
unwrap_or_else(Descr::any) as the fallback for missing entries,
which is the wrong direction (top instead of bottom) and produces
spurious widening — fz-3zx's runtime null-deref in
sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion traces back to this: the LFP
converges to sum returning int|float because iter-0 typed k_2's
body under Var(0)=any, producing arith Descr int|float that then
locked itself in.

## The fix

Introduce an explicit two-state value for the LFP table:

    pub enum InferredReturn {
        Unknown,
        Known(Descr),
    }

The LFP operates on HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), InferredReturn>:
  - Init every reachable spec to Unknown (not Descr::none()).
  - Type-rule propagation: any operation needing an Unknown input
    DEFERS — we don't call type_prim with an unknown operand. This
    parallels the existing \"spec not yet registered\" deferral at
    walk_spec_for_discovery:739.
  - LFP body: when joining contributions, Unknown contributes
    nothing (skip); Known(d) joins d. A spec stays Unknown until
    at least one exit path contributes a Known value.
  - Convergence: assert every reachable key is Known. Unknowns
    remaining ⇒ bug (we forgot to iterate).
  - Strip to HashMap<..., Descr> for ModuleTypes.effective_returns
    so downstream consumers (codegen, dump, cont_input_key) stay
    unchanged.

## Walker side

walk_spec_for_discovery's slot0 lookup gets the same treatment:
Unknown ⇒ no cont push (defer to next outer iter). Symmetric with
the existing \"specs.contains_key(&callee_key)\" defer.

## Why this is the right shape

  - Math: this IS the Kleene LFP, named honestly. \"Unknown\" is
    the lattice bottom for the iteration; \"none\" remains the
    lattice bottom for value sets. They are LOGICALLY distinct.
  - Engineering: end-of-LFP assertion catches non-convergence bugs
    that the any-fallback masked. type_prim never sees Unknown.
  - Removal: the unwrap_or_else(Descr::any) anti-pattern is
    deleted from every call/cont/tailcall lookup in the LFP.

## Acceptance

  - InferredReturn enum introduced in ir_typer.rs.
  - compute_effective_returns operates on InferredReturn,
    asserts post-convergence that all reachable keys are Known.
  - walk_spec_for_discovery's slot0 lookup defers on Unknown.
  - sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion passes with fz-3zx outer
    fixpoint applied on top.
  - All five golden specs converge to clean Descrs (sum's k_2 slot
    0 = int, not int|float).
  - fz-w5t (codegen seam reconciliation) becomes obsolete — close
    on landing this + fz-3zx.

## Order of work

  1. fz-3zx.1 (this) — InferredReturn + assertion. Lands without
     fz-3zx's outer fixpoint; defers any Unknown encountered in
     the current single-shot LFP run.
  2. fz-3zx — outer fixpoint + delete effective_return_descr.
  3. fz-w5t — close as obsoleted.
title: fz-3zx.1: InferredReturn — explicit Unknown/Known LFP table (precondition for fz-3zx)
